testcase.classname: chips.json loads at least one chip
testcase.name: chips.json loads at least one chip

testcase.classname: chips.json every chip has required string fields
testcase.name: chips.json every chip has required string fields

testcase.classname: chips.json every chip has valid numeric fields
testcase.name: chips.json every chip has valid numeric fields

testcase.classname: chips.json every chip has a non-empty processMix
testcase.name: chips.json every chip has a non-empty processMix

testcase.classname: chips.json chip IDs are unique
testcase.name: chips.json chip IDs are unique

testcase.classname: chips.json getChip returns correct chip by id
testcase.name: chips.json getChip returns correct chip by id

testcase.classname: chips.json getChip returns undefined for unknown id
testcase.name: chips.json getChip returns undefined for unknown id

testcase.classname: processes.json loads at least one process
testcase.name: processes.json loads at least one process

testcase.classname: processes.json every process has valid perStepPerWafer metrics
testcase.name: processes.json every process has valid perStepPerWafer metrics

testcase.classname: processes.json process IDs are unique
testcase.name: processes.json process IDs are unique

testcase.classname: processes.json getProcess returns correct process
testcase.name: processes.json getProcess returns correct process

testcase.classname: sources.json loads at least one source
testcase.name: sources.json loads at least one source

testcase.classname: sources.json every source has required fields
testcase.name: sources.json every source has required fields

testcase.classname: sources.json source IDs are unique
testcase.name: sources.json source IDs are unique

testcase.classname: sources.json getSource returns correct source
testcase.name: sources.json getSource returns correct source

testcase.classname: cross-reference integrity every chip.processMix references a known processId
testcase.name: cross-reference integrity every chip.processMix references a known processId

testcase.classname: cross-reference integrity every chip.specSourceId references a known sourceId (when set)
testcase.name: cross-reference integrity every chip.specSourceId references a known sourceId (when set)

testcase.classname: cross-reference integrity every process metric sourceId references a known source
testcase.name: cross-reference integrity every process metric sourceId references a known source

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — output shape returns all required fields
testcase.name: computeFootprint — output shape returns all required fields

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — output shape totals contain all four metrics plus ghgTotal
testcase.name: computeFootprint — output shape totals contain all four metrics plus ghgTotal

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — output shape ghgTotalKgCo2e = ghgScope2 + pfas (totals)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — output shape ghgTotalKgCo2e = ghgScope2 + pfas (totals)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) all per-die metrics are positive
testcase.name: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) all per-die metrics are positive

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) goodDiesPerWafer is positive for all chips
testcase.name: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) goodDiesPerWafer is positive for all chips

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) totalSteps is positive for all chips
testcase.name: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) totalSteps is positive for all chips

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) per-die water is less than per-wafer water
testcase.name: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) per-die water is less than per-wafer water

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) sourceIds is non-empty
testcase.name: computeFootprint — sanity ranges (per-die) sourceIds is non-empty

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) ref-n2 and ref-n28 exist in chips.json
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) ref-n2 and ref-n28 exist in chips.json

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 per-wafer GHG within ±20% of IMEC 1,600 kg CO2e target
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 per-wafer GHG within ±20% of IMEC 1,600 kg CO2e target

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 per-wafer water within ±20% of Hu2023 5,750 L target
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 per-wafer water within ±20% of Hu2023 5,750 L target

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 water scaling 1.8–3.0x (Bardon2020 target 2.3x)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 water scaling 1.8–3.0x (Bardon2020 target 2.3x)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 energy scaling 2.0–5.0x (Bardon2020 target 3.46x)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 energy scaling 2.0–5.0x (Bardon2020 target 3.46x)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 GHG scaling 1.8–4.0x (Bardon2020 target 2.5x)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N28→N2 GHG scaling 1.8–4.0x (Bardon2020 target 2.5x)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 Scope-2 share of total GHG is 40–80% (IMEC target ~60%)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — IMEC calibration (ref-n2, ref-n28) N2 Scope-2 share of total GHG is 40–80% (IMEC target ~60%)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — byFamily breakdown byFamily metrics sum ≤ perWafer totals (rounding noise allowed)
testcase.name: computeFootprint — byFamily breakdown byFamily metrics sum ≤ perWafer totals (rounding noise allowed)

testcase.classname: computeFootprint — byFamily breakdown byFamily keys are recognised process families
testcase.name: computeFootprint — byFamily breakdown byFamily keys are recognised process families

testcase.name: ConfidenceSchema accepts 'low'

testcase.name: ConfidenceSchema accepts 'estimated'

testcase.classname: ConfidenceSchema rejects unknown confidence
testcase.name: ConfidenceSchema rejects unknown confidence

testcase.name: ChipSchema accepts a valid chip

testcase.name: ChipSchema accepts a chip with optional fields

testcase.name: ChipSchema rejects yield outside [0,1]

testcase.name: ChipSchema rejects empty processMix

testcase.name: ChipSchema rejects missing required field

testcase.classname: ProcessSchema accepts a valid process
testcase.name: ProcessSchema accepts a valid process

testcase.classname: ProcessSchema rejects a negative metric value
testcase.name: ProcessSchema rejects a negative metric value

testcase.name: ProcessSchema rejects unknown family

testcase.classname: SourceSchema accepts a valid source
testcase.name: SourceSchema accepts a valid source

testcase.classname: SourceSchema rejects an invalid URL
testcase.name: SourceSchema rejects an invalid URL

testcase.classname: SourceSchema rejects an unknown type
testcase.name: SourceSchema rejects an unknown type

testcase.classname: SourceSchema accepts official_doc type
testcase.name: SourceSchema accepts official_doc type

testcase.classname: SourceSchema accepts optional fields (authors, keyFindings)
testcase.name: SourceSchema accepts optional fields (authors, keyFindings)

testcase.classname: ChipsFileSchema rejects chips array with duplicate IDs (cross-ref — schema allows, data.test catches)
testcase.name: ChipsFileSchema rejects chips array with duplicate IDs (cross-ref — schema allows, data.test catches)

testcase.classname: ProcessesFileSchema accepts a minimal processes file
testcase.name: ProcessesFileSchema accepts a minimal processes file

testcase.name: SourcesFileSchema accepts a minimal sources file

testcase.classname: waterToBathtubs very small amounts show bottles
testcase.name: waterToBathtubs very small amounts show bottles

testcase.classname: waterToBathtubs 1–9 bathtubs range
testcase.name: waterToBathtubs 1–9 bathtubs range

testcase.classname: waterToBathtubs ≥10 bathtubs returns rounded integer
testcase.name: waterToBathtubs ≥10 bathtubs returns rounded integer

testcase.classname: waterToHouseholdDays less than a day shows hours
testcase.name: waterToHouseholdDays less than a day shows hours

testcase.classname: waterToHouseholdDays multiple days
testcase.name: waterToHouseholdDays multiple days

testcase.classname: energyToACHours less than an hour shows minutes
testcase.name: energyToACHours less than an hour shows minutes

testcase.classname: energyToACHours hours range (1–23)
testcase.name: energyToACHours hours range (1–23)

testcase.classname: energyToACHours multiple days
testcase.name: energyToACHours multiple days

testcase.classname: energyToHouseholdDays less than a day shows hours
testcase.name: energyToHouseholdDays less than a day shows hours

testcase.classname: energyToHouseholdDays multiple days
testcase.name: energyToHouseholdDays multiple days

testcase.classname: ghgToCarKm less than 1 km shows metres
testcase.name: ghgToCarKm less than 1 km shows metres

testcase.classname: ghgToCarKm short distances show decimal km
testcase.name: ghgToCarKm short distances show decimal km

testcase.classname: ghgToCarKm long distances show rounded km
testcase.name: ghgToCarKm long distances show rounded km

testcase.classname: ghgToFlightFraction tiny fraction shows percentage with many decimals
testcase.name: ghgToFlightFraction tiny fraction shows percentage with many decimals

testcase.classname: ghgToFlightFraction partial flight shows percentage
testcase.name: ghgToFlightFraction partial flight shows percentage

testcase.classname: ghgToFlightFraction multiple flights shows multiplier
testcase.name: ghgToFlightFraction multiple flights shows multiplier

testcase.classname: formatMetric values ≥1000 use locale formatting
testcase.name: formatMetric values ≥1000 use locale formatting

testcase.classname: formatMetric values 100–999 use 0 decimal places by default
testcase.name: formatMetric values 100–999 use 0 decimal places by default

testcase.classname: formatMetric values 10–99 use 1 decimal place by default
testcase.name: formatMetric values 10–99 use 1 decimal place by default

testcase.classname: formatMetric values 1–9 use 1 decimal place by default
testcase.name: formatMetric values 1–9 use 1 decimal place by default

testcase.classname: formatMetric values 0.01–0.99 use 2 decimal places
testcase.name: formatMetric values 0.01–0.99 use 2 decimal places

testcase.classname: formatMetric very small values use 3 decimal places
testcase.name: formatMetric very small values use 3 decimal places

testcase.classname: formatMetric custom decimals override default
testcase.name: formatMetric custom decimals override default

testcase.name: getComparison waterL comparison is non-empty string

testcase.name: POST /api/revalidate GET /api/revalidate returns 405

testcase.name: POST /api/explain returns 400 for missing chipId

testcase.name: POST /api/explain returns 400 for unknown chipId

testcase.name: POST /api/chat returns 400 for missing required fields

testcase.name: POST /api/chat returns 400 for empty question

testcase.name: POST /api/chat returns 400 for question exceeding 2000 chars

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit first request is always allowed
testcase.name: checkRateLimit first request is always allowed

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit requests within the window are allowed up to MAX_REQUESTS (10)
testcase.name: checkRateLimit requests within the window are allowed up to MAX_REQUESTS (10)

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit 11th request within the window is rejected
testcase.name: checkRateLimit 11th request within the window is rejected

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit retryAfterMs is positive when rejected
testcase.name: checkRateLimit retryAfterMs is positive when rejected

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit requests reset after the window (60s) elapses
testcase.name: checkRateLimit requests reset after the window (60s) elapses

testcase.classname: checkRateLimit different IPs are tracked independently
testcase.name: checkRateLimit different IPs are tracked independently
